components.ai-prompt: Command renders a searchable command palette for keyboard-driven navigation and action execution. Use CommandDialog to wrap it in a modal opened by a keyboard shortcut (e.g. Cmd+K). Compose: Command (root) > CommandInput (search field) + CommandList > CommandGroup (sections with headings) > CommandItem (each action). CommandEmpty renders when no items match the search. CommandSeparator adds a visual divider between groups. CommandShortcut renders a keyboard hint aligned to the right of a CommandItem. Filter is built in — items are hidden automatically based on CommandInput value.

aria: Icon is decorative; no alt text needed
Action button is keyboard-accessible
accessibility.notes: The icon is rendered inside a decorative div. Title uses H5 typography. Description uses Body typography. The action button is a standard Button and is fully keyboard-accessible.

components.ai-prompt: EmptyState is an empty-state placeholder component for surfaces that have no data. Use it inside tables, lists, or panels when there is no content to display. It centers an optional icon, a required title, an optional description, and an optional call-to-action button vertically. Key props: icon (ReactNode, decorative icon), title (string, required), description (string), action ({ label: string; onClick: () => void }). Import as EmptyState from @ds-foundation/react.

aria: role=button on the card root
aria-pressed reflecting the selected state
aria-disabled reflecting the disabled state
tabIndex=-1 when disabled to remove from tab order
accessibility.notes: Activated by Enter or Space key in addition to click. Disabled cards are removed from tab order via tabIndex=-1. Selection indicator (radio or checkbox) is purely visual — ARIA state is carried by aria-pressed on the card root.

components.ai-prompt: FormCard is a selectable card component for radio-style or checkbox-style option selection in forms. Use it when users need to pick from a set of options presented as cards rather than native radio/checkbox inputs — for example, payment method selection, plan tiers, or configuration presets. It supports two layouts: tall (stacked, default) with the indicator and icon on top, and long (horizontal) with the indicator on the left. Key props: label (required), description, icon (ReactNode), selected (boolean), disabled (boolean), selectionType ('radio' | 'checkbox', default 'radio'), layout ('tall' | 'long', default 'tall'), onClick. Export: FormCard.

default.base: space-y-2

aria: aria-invalid on FormControl when field has an error
aria-describedby linking FormControl to FormDescription and FormMessage
FormLabel htmlFor is wired automatically to the field's generated id
FormMessage is conditionally rendered only when error or children exist
accessibility.notes: Built on React Hook Form's FormProvider and Controller. FormField wraps Controller and provides field context. useFormField hook exposes id, name, and field state for custom field implementations. FormLabel turns red (text-destructive) on error automatically.

components.ai-prompt: Form is a React Hook Form integration layer built on Radix UI label primitives. Use it to build validated, accessible forms. Wrap the react-hook-form useForm instance in Form (re-export of FormProvider), then use FormField to connect each field to the form, wrapping the input in FormItem > FormLabel + FormControl + FormDescription + FormMessage. FormControl handles aria-invalid, aria-describedby, and id wiring automatically. useFormField is available for custom field components. Key exports: Form, FormField, FormItem, FormLabel, FormControl, FormDescription, FormMessage, useFormField.

accessibility.notes: The aria-live='polite' region is always in the DOM even when state is 'fresh' (rendered as display:none) to prevent screen reader announcement interruptions on mount.

components.ai-prompt: FreshnessChip shows data recency status: fresh (hidden), watch (amber, 5–15 min old), stale (red, 15+ min). Pass state and timestamp; the component formats the relative age automatically. Exports deriveFreshnessState(lastUpdatedAt) utility to compute state from a Date. Stale state optionally shows a refresh button.

default.base: flex items-center gap-1

aria: role=spinbutton on the inner input
aria-valuemin reflecting the min prop
aria-valuemax reflecting the max prop
aria-valuenow reflecting the current numeric value
aria-label='Decrease' on the decrement button
aria-label='Increase' on the increment button
accessibility.notes: The inner text input carries role=spinbutton with aria-valuemin, aria-valuemax, and aria-valuenow. Decrement/increment buttons use ghost icon button style and are disabled when the value is at its bound. Value is clamped on blur. Supports both controlled and uncontrolled usage. precision prop controls decimal places.

components.ai-prompt: InputNumber is a numeric stepper input with decrement and increment ghost icon buttons. Use it for quantity selectors, page counts, threshold values, or any bounded integer or decimal input. It supports controlled (value + onChange) and uncontrolled (defaultValue) modes, min/max clamping, configurable step and precision. The inner input uses role=spinbutton with aria-valuemin/max/now. Key props: value (number | undefined), defaultValue (number | undefined), onChange ((value: number | undefined) => void), min, max, step (default 1), precision (default 0). Export: InputNumber.

default.base: flex items-center gap-2 has-[:disabled]:opacity-50

aria: InputOTPSeparator has role=separator
Active slot is indicated via ring-2 ring-ring ring-offset-background
Disabled state applies opacity-50 via has-[:disabled] CSS selector on the container
accessibility.notes: Built on the input-otp library. The underlying element is a single invisible native input — screen readers interact with it as a single text field. Slot rendering (InputOTPSlot) is purely visual. InputOTPGroup groups slots visually. InputOTPSeparator renders a Dot icon with role=separator between groups.

components.ai-prompt: InputOTP is a one-time password input with individual character slots built on the input-otp library. Use it for verification codes, PIN entry, or any fixed-length code entry. Compose InputOTPGroup containing InputOTPSlot elements (one per character), with optional InputOTPSeparator between groups. Key props on InputOTP: maxLength (number, required), pattern (string, for allowed characters — e.g. REGEXP_ONLY_DIGITS). The index prop on InputOTPSlot specifies which character position the slot displays. Exports: InputOTP, InputOTPGroup, InputOTPSlot, InputOTPSeparator.

components.ai-prompt: Input is a styled text input field wrapping a native HTML input. Use it for all single-line text entry including email, password, search, and URL fields. Always pair with a Label or provide aria-label. When used inside the Form component set, FormControl wires all ARIA automatically. Export: Input (component), InputProps (TypeScript interface).

components.ai-prompt: Label is an accessible form label built on the Radix UI Label primitive. Use it to label any form control — Input, Select, Textarea, Checkbox — by setting htmlFor to the input's id. It renders a native <label> element in ripple-blue-90 text at 14px/medium weight. The peer-disabled variant automatically dims the label when the sibling input is disabled. When used inside the Form component set, FormLabel handles the htmlFor wiring automatically. Export: Label.

aria-label or aria-live region to announce when content loads in
accessibility.notes: Skeleton itself has no ARIA role. Wrap loading regions in a container with aria-busy="true" and aria-label describing what is loading. Remove aria-busy when content replaces skeletons.

components.ai-prompt: Skeleton is a pulsing loading placeholder that mimics the shape of content before it loads. Use it to prevent layout shift and reduce perceived wait time. Render one or more Skeleton elements shaped like the content they replace — set width and height via className (e.g., h-4 w-3/4 for a text line, h-12 w-12 rounded-full for an avatar). Replace skeletons with real content once data is available. For screen reader support, wrap a skeleton group in a container with aria-busy="true" and aria-label="Loading...". There are no variants or props beyond className.

aria: role="slider" on each thumb
aria-valuemin, aria-valuemax, aria-valuenow on each thumb
aria-label or aria-labelledby on root or individual thumbs
aria-orientation="horizontal" (default) or "vertical"
accessibility.notes: Supports single and range (multi-thumb) values. Arrow keys move thumb by step; Page Up/Down move by larger increments; Home/End jump to min/max.

components.ai-prompt: Slider is a range input control built on Radix UI for selecting a numeric value (or range) within a defined min–max. Use it for volume controls, price range filters, opacity pickers, or any continuous value selection. Pass value (array for range) and onValueChange for controlled usage, or defaultValue for uncontrolled. min defaults to 0, max to 100, step to 1. For a range slider pass two values: [20, 80]. The track fills from the leftmost thumb to the rightmost, coloured with bg-primary. Always provide an aria-label on the root for screen readers.

default.base: toaster group

aria: role=status on individual toasts for polite announcements
role=alert used by sonner for error toasts (assertive)
accessibility.notes: Toasts are announced by screen readers via live regions managed by the sonner library. Error toasts use assertive, others use polite.

components.ai-prompt: Sonner is a toast notification system built on the sonner library, pre-configured with design system theming and Lucide icons (CircleCheck for success, Info, TriangleAlert for warning, OctagonX for error, LoaderCircle for loading). Place a single Toaster instance near the root of your app, then trigger toasts from anywhere using the sonner toast() API. Accepts all sonner Toaster props for customisation. Exports: Toaster.

default.base: animate-spin

aria: role=status on the outer span
aria-label='Loading' on the outer span
sr-only span with text 'Loading…' for screen readers
accessibility.notes: The visible SVG is purely decorative. All screen reader communication is via the sr-only text inside the role=status wrapper.

components.ai-prompt: Spinner is a simple animated loading indicator using an SVG circle with a dash-array animation. Use it to signal asynchronous activity in buttons, empty states, and overlay loaders. Size is controlled by the size prop (sm=16px, md=24px, lg=32px). The component is accessible by default with role=status and sr-only text. Exports: Spinner.

aria: role=switch on the root element (set by Radix)
aria-checked reflects the checked state
aria-disabled when disabled prop is set
accessibility.notes: Always pair with a visible Label component. Radix manages role=switch and aria-checked automatically. Space key toggles the switch.

components.ai-prompt: Switch is a binary on/off toggle built on Radix UI Switch primitive. It reflects checked/unchecked state via data-[state] attributes and handles keyboard toggling (Space). Always pair it with a visible Label or wrap in a form field for accessibility. Use for settings toggles, feature flags, and notification preferences. Controlled via checked and onCheckedChange props. Exports: Switch.

default.base: w-full caption-bottom text-sm

aria: Semantic HTML table elements (table, thead, tbody, tfoot, tr, th, td, caption) carry implicit ARIA roles
Use TableCaption to provide an accessible table title
th elements in TableHead provide column header context for screen readers
accessibility.notes: Uses native HTML table semantics. No additional ARIA roles needed. Wrap with TableCaption for an accessible name. The root Table component wraps in a scrollable div for horizontal overflow.

components.ai-prompt: Table is a composable data table built from semantic HTML elements. Assemble it with Table (root), TableHeader, TableBody, TableFooter, TableHead (th), TableRow, TableCell (td), and TableCaption. The root Table is wrapped in a horizontally scrollable container automatically. Use for structured data display such as transaction lists, reports, and dashboards.
